_note: Vikram's employer processed under New Regime. His ₹50,000 referral bonus appears as 'other income' in Part B. HRA received (₹3.6L) but Vikram did not submit rent receipts to employer. Home loan interest (₹2.4L) not claimed with employer. ELSS SIPs (₹96k) not submitted to employer. LIC premiums (₹37.5k) not submitted. NPS voluntary (₹1.5L under 80CCD-1B) not submitted. Employer NPS contribution is NIL (Infosys BPM does not offer NPS scheme).

exemptions._note: Employee did not submit rent receipts or LTA claims to employer

part_b._note: Employee did NOT declare any other income to employer. Freelance, crypto, equity, FD interest — all undeclared to employer.

deductions_chapter_vi_a._note: New Regime selected. Most deductions not applicable. 80CCD(2) is NIL as employer doesn't contribute to NPS. Employee's own NPS top-up (80CCD-1B, 1.5L) not submitted.

AIS_not_in_Form16: FD interest ₹35,000 (Axis Bank) — NOT in Form 16
Crypto VDA ₹4,99,000 proceeds — NOT in Form 16
ELSS MF redemption ₹1,25,000 — NOT in Form 16
deductions_not_claimed_but_eligible: 80CCD-1B: NPS voluntary ₹1,50,000 — NOT CLAIMED (New Regime blocks this)
80C: ELSS SIPs ₹96,000 + LIC ₹37,500 = ₹1,33,500 — NOT CLAIMED (New Regime blocks this)
If Old Regime: HRA exemption possible — rent ₹22k/month, basic ₹1,04,880/month, metro city
If Old Regime: Section 24(b) home loan interest ~₹2,40,000 — NOT CLAIMED
80D: Health insurance premium ₹24,000 (Star Health) — NOT CLAIMED (New Regime blocks)
_what_auditor_should_find.regime_analysis: Under New Regime: taxable = 2,544,600 + undeclared income. Under Old Regime: with HRA ₹2.1L exemption + 24b ₹2L + 80C ₹1.5L + 80D ₹25k + 80CCD-1B ₹50k: net deductions ≈ ₹6.1L. May be optimal to switch regimes despite higher declared income. Calculator will determine.
_what_auditor_should_find.notice_risk: CRITICAL — crypto (largest trigger), freelance undeclared, equity sales undeclared, FD/savings interest undeclared. Multiple AIS entries with no corresponding Form 16 declaration.
